title: The language frontend is presentation-only
summary: C, C++, and CUDA source-buffer bytes are never rewritten merely to obtain the frontend spelling.
details: Presentation is implemented with conceal, inline or overlay virtual text, highlights, folds, and window-local matches.
Compilers, formatters, LSP clients, git, and debugger source locations must continue to observe the real source.
Editing features such as snippets are separate authoring tools and are not presentation transforms.
rationale: A visual language can be aggressive only if downstream tools retain a single authoritative source representation.

provenance.note: Owner described the frontend as a custom Neovim presentation of real C++ code.
Current frontend modules decorate through Neovim display APIs rather than nvim_buf_set_text or nvim_buf_set_lines.

evidence.claim: Visual reveal and restore transitions leave every source line byte-identical.
Rendering uses namespaces, extmarks, conceal, virtual text, highlights, and folds.

questions: Can the frontend rewrite buffer text?
Do clangd and git see the Odin-like spelling?
Which APIs may presentation modules use?

last_verified.method: Static API inventory plus interaction source-integrity assertions.
